stories: Good evening Sean, it's Friday evening, the first of May, 2026
Here's a quietly lovely one to start your evening: when Apollo 16 astronaut Charles Duke walked on the moon back in 1972, he left behind a small family photo of himself, his wife, and his two boys
It's still up there, fifty-four years later, sitting in the lunar dust where no wind will ever move it
Your calendar is clear for tomorrow, so Saturday looks fully open
On home turf, Prime Minister Lawrence Wong delivered his May Day Rally today, and the headline line was a sharp one
As AI reshapes whole industries, he said, the government cannot promise to protect every job, but it will commit to protecting every worker
The labour movement also pledged thirty-seven million dollars this year to help workers and their families adapt
Worth listening to in full when you have a moment
Good morning Sean, it's Friday, the first of May, 2026
And here's a small one to start your morning: dolphins call each other by name
Each one develops a unique signature whistle as a calf, and other dolphins use that exact whistle to address that specific individual â even years after their last contact
It's the only confirmed example, outside of humans, of an animal recognising a personal name
A pretty light one for you today, given Singapore is officially down for Labour Day
Otherwise, a quiet rhythm to ease into the long weekend
NTUC's running its usual Downtown East celebrations alongside, with performances, lucky draws, and free ice cream at islandwide locations
Worth tracking â it shapes the trade backdrop for everyone exporting commodities into Europe
Good evening Sean, it's Thursday evening, the thirtieth of April, 2026
Here's an oddly perfect one for tonight: hippos cannot actually swim
Their bones are so dense they're barely buoyant, so what they do instead is a kind of slow-motion gallop along the riverbed, pushing off the mud like underwater astronauts
Tomorrow is Friday the first of May, which is Labour Day in Singapore, so the city is officially down
You've still got a few work touchpoints though
Otherwise, the public holiday gives you a bit of breathing room
On home turf, Prime Minister Lawrence Wong delivers his May Day Rally speech tomorrow, with a focus on helping workers adapt as new technologies reshape job roles
One to listen for, given how directly it touches the labour market you operate in
Good morning Sean, it's Thursday, the thirtieth of April, 2026
There are more trees on Earth than there are stars in our entire galaxy
Roughly three trillion trees against the Milky Way's couple hundred billion stars
And below the soil, those trees actually talk to one another through fungal networks the scientists call the wood wide web, sending chemical messages about drought, disease, and even insect attacks
So next time you walk past a forest, you're really walking past a quiet conversation
Saskatoon to Toronto is already behind you, the connection across to Seattle later this morning, and then the long haul back to Singapore in the evening
In between, you've got the spares risk follow-up at five thirty SGT, the PMO connect at six, and the Hypercare stabilisation forum at seven thirty Singapore time to round things off
The Marriott near Sea-Tac is the one fixed bed of the day before you're back in the air
